level_2.origins_history:
Aligoté is an ancient Burgundian variety, the offspring of Pinot Noir and Gouais Blanc, making it a full sibling to Chardonnay. Despite this noble parentage, Aligoté was historically considered a peasant grape, relegated to lesser vineyard sites while Chardonnay claimed the limestone slopes.

The variety was traditionally used for Kir, a cocktail of white wine and crème de cassis. In recent decades, a new generation of producers has championed Aligoté as a serious wine, particularly from the village of Bouzeron, which received its own appellation in 1998.

genetic_lineage.overview: Aligoté shares identical parentage with Chardonnay, making them full siblings. Both are children of Pinot Noir and Gouais Blanc.

parents.name: Pinot Noir
parents.notes: Confirmed through DNA analysis. Aligoté inherits some of Pinot Noir's aromatic potential.

parents.name: Gouais Blanc
parents.notes: An ancient, high-yielding variety that contributed vigor and acidity to many of its offspring.

siblings.name: Chardonnay
siblings.shared_parent: Both Pinot Noir and Gouais Blanc
siblings.notes: Chardonnay and Aligoté are full siblings, though Chardonnay achieved far greater fame.

siblings.name: Gamay
siblings.shared_parent: Both Pinot Noir and Gouais Blanc
siblings.notes: Another full sibling, the red grape of Beaujolais.

siblings.name: Melon de Bourgogne
siblings.shared_parent: Both Pinot Noir and Gouais Blanc
siblings.notes: The grape of Muscadet, also a full sibling.

genetic_lineage.genetic_significance: Aligoté demonstrates that identical parentage can produce very different grapes. While Chardonnay became the world's most popular white variety, Aligoté remained a regional specialty.

terroir.effect: The best Aligoté sites in Bouzeron feature clay-limestone soils that provide drainage while retaining enough water for the variety's needs.

susceptibilities.notes: Aligoté is notably susceptible to downy mildew, requiring preventive treatments in humid conditions.
susceptibilities.impact_on_wine: Reduced yields, potential off-flavors if untreated

susceptibilities.notes: Susceptible to botrytis bunch rot, particularly in wet vintages. Requires careful canopy management.
susceptibilities.impact_on_wine: Crop loss, though noble rot is not typically sought for this variety

disease_profile.general_notes: Aligoté is vigorous and early budding, making it susceptible to spring frosts. Early ripening helps avoid autumn rot in most years.

regional_expressions.style_summary: The only appellation dedicated to Aligoté. Produces the finest, most serious examples with mineral depth and aging potential.

typical_character.aromatics: Citrus, green apple, white flowers, almond, mineral

regional_expressions.style_summary: Regional Burgundy appellation for Aligoté grown throughout the region. Quality varies widely; best examples are fresh and mineral.

typical_character.aromatics: Lemon, green apple, sometimes slightly bitter almond

regional_expressions.style_summary: Moldova has the largest Aligoté plantings in the world, with nearly 16,000 hectares. The variety thrives here, producing wines with better balance than Burgundy due to warmer ripening conditions.
Ukraine's second largest white variety, with nearly 10,000 hectares planted. Often blended with other varieties, occasionally made into sweet wines.
Planted primarily in the Moldova region of eastern Romania, with over 7,000 hectares. Produces crisp, everyday whites.
